items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved an ordinance updating business license tax rates in Campbell's municipal code. The changes take effect in the next fiscal year and could affect local business operating costs.

items.headline: Affordable housing restrictions policy deferred for further review
items.summary: The City Council postponed discussion of a policy addressing affordability restrictions when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure until April 28. This affects long-term stability of affordable housing in San José.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves giant signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: The city authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium to celebrate FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This is a visible change residents will notice during the tournament period.

items.headline: Milpitas updates development fees and user charges for FY 2026-27
items.summary: The City Council approved updated development fees and various user charges for the upcoming fiscal year. These changes affect costs for residents and businesses seeking permits and city services.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness plan at Milpitas Council
items.summary: County officials presented the Community Plan to End Homelessness to the City Council, indicating regional efforts to address homelessness in the area. This could affect local services and policies Milpitas implements.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development adds 4 new homes to Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four single-family homes permitted on Altino Boulevard as part of the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 project, ranging from $487k-$573k. This represents continued infill development in an established residential area.

items.headline: City releases guide to building permits and development timelines
items.summary: San José published a resource explaining the permitting process, timelines, and coordination needed to develop projects in the city. This could help residents and developers understand how long construction projects take and what to expect.

items.headline: City updates climate action plan toward 2028 goals
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed progress on the Climate Action Playbook and shared an updated 'Game Plan 2028' for FY 2025-26. This outlines Sunnyvale's next steps on environmental commitments residents have been following.
